transcript.text: Hi, everyone.
Hi, how's it going?
I am good.
How are you?
All good here.
I'm still waiting for Nathan and Drew.
Hello.
Hi.
How are y'all today?
Good.
How are you?
Well, thank you.
It snowed here yesterday, so today's the first day where you can kind of like feel like it's winter's coming.
Where are you?
I am based in Sarajevo, Bosnia.
We're like southern, like southeast Europe.
But we're surrounded by mountains, so the winters can get pretty cold.
Oh, yeah.
I know all about that.
I live 5,500 feet above sea level.
The mountains just to my west over here are, you know, the bases of them are 10,000 feet above sea level.
So we're in full-blown ski mode now.
It's been slow season.
Are you in Denver?
Yeah, just outside of Denver.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Yeah.
It's been a slow start.
We finally got, like, a big snow earlier this week, but not too much yet.
Yeah.
I remember when I was growing up, we used to get a lot more snow than we do right now.
Hi, Andrew.
Hello.
I don't believe that we've met yet.
I think I, if you were at the kickoff, I joined a week after.
Before the kickoff, but I'm an editorial account strategist at.
Growthx.
So I'm in charge of developing the content strategy and kind of lead the content production process alongside Erik.
Great.
Nice to meet you.
Normally, this is at a time that I can't attend and whatever.
Things changed this week, so I'm able to be here.
Excited to hear what's going on.
Cool.
Perfect.
Well, let me hop in.
I'm going to share my screen.
I've added everyone to the agenda already.

So to give you a couple of updates, so on Monday, we met with the PMM team to discuss reviewing the inputs that go into our article generation process, specifically the company context and the audience personas.
Garrett has already reviewed the company context.
He has left feedback, and he has also uploaded internal documentation for the co-product.
And I'm waiting on other PMMs to review.
Just from the point of view of the other products, I will send a follow-up email today to make sure we're on track to get that done by the end of the week.
I did start working on the product feature matrix just using the documentation that Garrett has sent.
So this one just focuses on Cove, and it contains a product data sheet.
And then it also summarizes how Cove helps companies meet backup requirements for cyber insurance.
And there's also a quick comparison with some of your top competitors.
However, some of these points, they're clearly intended for salespeople.
So I would remove specific call-outs regarding forced three-year contracts and frequent account manager changes.
in for relations.

Just because we don't want this to be mentioned in the content, I think it's a little too, it's not something that typically gets brought up in blog content, but, you know, the points around how data is more expensive, and, you know, around, like, distributed workloads, those are all things that we could kind of bring up in content.

And my plan was to do this for every single, like, for the other products that we are also talking about, and then we can use this document, once we generate an article draft, we can use this document to fact check the draft against this document and make sure that the product messaging is correct, and that we are kind of presenting the benefits in an accurate way.
Um, and then...
Once, this week, I'm also, I mean, today, I'm going to give access, like, editing access to Airtable to Elaine.

I think that would be, like, from, I think if everyone agrees, so Elaine can have marketing, like, access for the marketing team, and then I think it would be helpful if Studi would have access to represent, like, the PMMs.
But if there are any other people that you think should have editing access, let me know, and I can send an invite.
Question for you all.
Have you done any keyword research on the term business resilience in conjunction with cyber resilience?
Not yet, no.

I think that is one that we should definitely do some research on for the next round, too, because, I don't know, Andrew Beyer, correct me if I'm wrong here, are we, like, positioning as business resiliency, cyber resiliency?
It's not good.
Is clear to me?
it the same thing?
Yeah, mean, right now we're saying business resilience.
Like that, that's what we're saying.
I think from our perspective, they're effectively the same thing.
But I don't know, like a lot of people were talking about cyber resilience, not a lot of people talking about business resilience.
So I guess they saw it as a way to differentiate.
Yeah, I think for when we're doing keyword research, we typically avoid targeting keywords that have the same search results.
So if the same websites show up for two different keywords, that means that the intent is basically the same.
So we avoid targeting them with different types of pieces of content.
For business resilience and cyber resilience, my hunch is that the thing
Search results are going to be different, so we can fold those in.
Even though they're quite similar in essence, I think we can target both of these concepts.
Okay.
Yeah.
You just mentioned giving Studi access to Airtable.
She's going to be out for the next two weeks, so I wonder if we need updates in Airtable in less time than that, we should probably find somebody else to do it.
Okay.
Okay.
That's fine.
I think I can then give the PMMs access that we met with on Monday.
Garrett.
Joe, Garrett, and Jen?
Yeah.
Okay.
Yeah.
I think that sounds like a good plan to us, and then my idea was to, I mean, at first, I actually wanted to ask, I know that you might have, you were.
Yeah.
Last week we were talking a little bit about reviewing this strategy like internally.
Andrew, I know that you mentioned that you might like have some additions to the strategy.
So I just wanted to check and see if that was something that you were able to do since last week.
Yeah, so we haven't been able to gather internally to review everything.
That's sort of why I'm hoping the PMMs can have access to Airtable or the Content OS specifically, just so that they can leave their feedback in there.
I don't think there's been any true like group conversation about whether these five clusters are exactly what we'd like to put the time towards.
And I know that that's something that we're trying to determine during this strategy sprint.
And also why I asked if we're able to iterate on it, you know, outside of these next two weeks, because there might be some...
You know, some further thoughts.
So that's something that's on our end.
I think the PMMs are trying to get any documentation together first by, I think, hopefully tomorrow.
So we have all of those documents for you, you know, outside of just Cove.
And my hope is the following week, there can be feedback on the topics and the clusters themselves.
Yeah, yeah, that totally works.
I think for, as like we, usually we tend to do additional keyword research on a like monthly basis or maybe every six weeks, every six to eight weeks.
So it's totally fine if we get additional feedback this week or next week.
It's like the content OS isn't, is like an iterative document.
So any feedback that they leave around the topic, it's like, we shouldn't do this, but let's look into like another topic.
That's totally fine.
And we can get on board with that.
And we do.
Work with product teams like that.
So we do have a couple of clients that, you know, like their PMs leave feedback on the topics and they also review the draft.
So it's not an issue.
That's great.
Yeah, no.
And that's not to say I don't think any of these five are like strong ideas.
I just want to make sure that it's reviewed internally and we have, you know, our product team and potentially campaigns and such also weigh it.
And seeing as how we, you know, limited amount of content that you'll help us to produce, just want to make sure that everyone is giving the thumbs up.
Right.
So.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Okay.
All right.
So I was just thinking about what would be the best way to.
To, so we do, we did deliver five drafts so far.
And.
I think we want to wait until the PMMs provide their feedback this week, and then we're going to go in and just edit them using the latest product information that they've given us.
And then we can ping you again to do another round of review.
Does that sound like a plan, like a good plan for you?
Yeah, I personally think, too, that one topic that is able to be associated with all of our products is going to be the most helpful.

Like, don't think multiple calibration pieces at this point is, like, just one topic that speaks to all products so that there's buy-in from the PMMs that their products are being referenced correctly and that there aren't any hallucinations and stuff would be the most helpful.
Okay, okay, that works as well.
I think we can, we'll go in and pick one that's probably, like, the best, that mentions all of them, where we can, um...
Make sure that that's accurate.
I did add a new status here that says PMM review so we can separate them because the draft review is just something that we have sent to you, but we haven't heard from the PMMs.
Okay, so that's a sounds like a plan.

And then I am going to share access with the PMMs and I will just send another email and let them know that they can go in and provide feedback on the topics like they don't have to wait until like, you know, they're done reviewing the content, like the company context artifact, they can go in and like review the topics right away.
That's perfect.
Yeah, and I'll help to push on that internally.

So we get our ducks in a row, hopefully before the end of next week, I'd like to have, you know, the end of this week, all documentation that we'd like to provide, forward it over and then end of next week, I'm hoping for some concrete feedback on the current topic list.
Okay, okay, perfect.

And then I think, okay, we'll try to, depending on when we get the feedback from them, I think we can edit an article maybe on Friday and send it over because I would just, I'm just thinking about when to resume content production because we are pausing right now until we get all of the documents from them.
But I think I'm hoping that next week we can kind of resume with production.
Okay.
All right, good.
Did you guys have any other questions?
I think those are all the updates that I had in terms of what we've been doing internally.
So, like I said, I started building the product feature matrix.
I'm going to continue building it out as we get additional documents.
But I was curious if you had any other questions.
Keep me posted on whether or not the other PMMs.
PMMs.
Give you all feedback.
can push internally.
Okay.
Okay.
Did they commit to end of week?
Yes.
Yeah.
Okay.
I guess if we don't have it by Friday morning, please let me know and I will escalate aggressively.
Okay.
Thank you.
Yeah.

think for me, it's just important that if I can like help provide any additional context for what we're trying to do, I feel free to like ask if I, you know, if you need me to send an email or anything, because I think what we're trying to do with organic content can sometimes be a little bit, sometimes we get feedback, like, why are we doing this specific topic?
Like, what's the benefit here?
Because sometimes, you know, from...

From a product marketing perspective, sometimes it doesn't make sense to be talking about a general topic in cybersecurity because they think, well, we should just be talking about our product here and we're trying to achieve like a different set of goals.
So I think the strategy document does explain what we're trying to do well, but if there's anything else that you need to provide, you can let me know.
Question for me, I guess a couple.
These new documents that the PMMs are looking to produce, these matrix documents, where in the content production process will they be referenced?
Are they like as a sort of more of a check near the end or is it like, you know, a few different times?
I'm just trying to understand.
I know you showed that timeline of all the different steps and the agents and I'm just curious to know like where these will be used.
Yeah, so right now where we're planning.
We plan to use them once an article draft is generated, and we will implement a product fact-checking step that takes this feature matrix and then grades the content against the feature matrix.
And any product mentions that aren't consistent with the artifact, they will be amended.
However, the documentation that we've received is also helpful from a topic selection perspective.

So now we have like a better insight into the different aspects of, for example, of Cove and like what are the different features and functionalities, you know, how like it assists with disaster recovery, for example.
So I can use like all of these different features, I can use them.
To like start doing like keyword research.
So in a way it can also like feed into future topic selection and it makes it also easier for us to link articles to different products.
So that, you know, the majority of the articles that we're producing are linked to individual products in some way.
So those are the two ways that I am planning on using these.
Okay.
No, that's helpful to understand.
Thank you.

And the other is based on other work that you've done with, I suppose, similar clients or product teams, do you find that the product team gives feedback and then a portion of the content is re-prompted or re-run?
Or are you finding that clients are just changing things on their end and then running with it as is?
Like I'm wondering if there's going to be like a feedback loop and then it adds like, you know, a bit to the process.
Yeah.
So what we do is if there sometimes, there are some pieces of feedback that are just topic specific.

So for example, we might have a PM provide feedback on how we're framing a specific, for example, if we're talking about a specific product and we are mentioning it as a solution to a specific problem, they might have a better example of that problem.
So they might ask like, hey, like instead of mentioning this like cybersecurity risk, let's actually talk about this other cybersecurity risk that's more relevant for this product.
So those types of comments, we can use them, add them back into the product feature matrix and in the company context.
Sometimes there are comments that are more, I wouldn't say surface level.
But it's something that we can resolve and then the editor can just kind of make a note in their internal documentation to watch out for that in the future.
So we do kind of make sure to go back in and update everything.
Right now our article generation pipeline doesn't have the ability to kind of learn from feedback, but we can do that by updating the inputs on a consistent basis.
Okay.
Yeah, no, that's, that is helpful as well.
Okay.
Thank you for that.
Yeah, yeah, sure.
And I think we've had like a, it was actually, I think yesterday that we had like a PM review a blog post for a different company.
And they had a suggestion around, like sometimes they might have a suggestion around the angle of a specific article.
And in that case, that type of feedback would just impact.
That's.
Thank
That specific article.
we wouldn't like be implementing it in the artifacts.
So like it's, we assess it on a case by case basis.
Okay.
All right.
Great.
So action items for me, I will do keyword research around business resilience.
I will upload those topics to the content calendar.
I will give everyone editing access to Airtable.
So you can start, like the PMMs can start leaving comments on the article suggestions.
And then I will send a reminder to the other PMMs to review the artifacts today.
If you, if you think that's okay, if you want to do that yourself, that's totally fine.
Okay.
All right.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Sorry.
I just have a quick question because I noticed you use, um, can by Bye.
disclaimers on all of the drafts for AI use.
I'm just wondering, like, in your experience, I'm assuming you use this for all of AI-generated content.
I'm just wondering if, in your experience, does it have any impact with engagement or stuff like that?
Yeah.
No, I think with, we don't use them for all of the clients that we work with.
We did incorporate a disclaimer for Enable because the AI style guide that you provided, it does include instructions to include, like, a disclaimer at the end.
Because it comes at the end and not at the beginning, it should not impact engagement.
Typically, what impacts user engagement with blog posts, it's the structure and its readability.
So, if an article is skimmable, if the HTML HTML HTML skimmable,
H2s and H3s are clear and direct and the person can find what they're looking for quickly, then it usually leads to a more engaged session.
But I don't think the disclaimer should have an impact.
We have another client who has, we lead disclaimers also at the end of the article that basically says, like, this is not legal advice.
So it hasn't really impacted engagement or traffic in any way.
Yeah.
Thanks.
Yeah, Andrew.
Just one final question.
In the content OS, I know we have cluster descriptions.
I don't know if there's something comparable for each of the proposed topics.

Like if there's in your backend engines somewhere, if there's like an article brief or like a topic brief or something, I think it would be helpful for PMM and others when they're scanning this topic list.
And they're
coming into consideration if there's a little bit more context behind the direction of the proposed content.

So, you know, even if that's just like this topic will speak to enables X and Y products, so it's like easier to understand what will be referenced or what might be referenced or like the direction that the content could be seen to go.
So, I'm not sure if that field currently exists somewhere, but if that could be added to the table, that'd be really helpful, I think.
Yeah, yeah, we can add that, like a short description of what the article could cover.
Yeah, I think I could do that.
Yeah, yeah, just again, so it's like known what to expect or there could be like someone scanning this, could be very easy to be like, okay, not the most relevant to our audience.
Or like, if it was written with this intent, it might be more specific to like the user base that we're trying to target, essentially.
Or, you know, maybe...
maybe...
Maybe the products wouldn't be exactly how we want to position.
It's just those things I think would be helpful versus just the proposed titles and the primary keywords because I think some of them could be fairly broad.
Yeah, yeah, I got it.
No, I can add that in tomorrow.
Sure.
And then I will let you know.
Yeah, that would just be super helpful.
And yeah, if you're able to by not even necessarily tomorrow, but end of week so that when we review internally that that context can be there, that'd be very helpful.
Yeah.
All right.
That sounds good.
I have a couple of things.
We should remove the disclaimer from the style guide.
Like just delete it if possible.
Like nobody cares.
If you care, you care.
If you don't care, most people don't care.
Let's just remove it.
Uh.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Okay.
So there's that.
Anybody have concerns about that?
No, I was just going to say, Andrew, there's a conversation, I think, internally right now with Legal, if they want to keep it.
I'm personally extremely indifferent to it, so I don't think we need to.
Well, let's remove it.
If Legal overrides us later, then they override us later.
But we've got AI-generated content on the website that I don't think has that disclaimer on it, so whatever.

The second thing is, when you structure the content, is there any consideration for how other LLMs might consume the data to try to amplify the chat GPTs of the world, picking it up and leveraging it?
Yes.
So, there are a couple of things that we've been...
Implementing in content to make it more discoverable by LLMs.
So one of those things is a frequently asked questions section at the end of every article.
That seems to be, in our experience, it does get picked up by LLMs a lot.
It also sometimes helps to add like a quick, like a TLDR, quick insights box, like with a couple of bullet points summarizing the article under the intro or right after the intro.
And then the actual content itself, how it's written.
So LLMs have a tendency to cite content that is already kind of well-structured in the sense that every section talks about a specific idea and every paragraph is unique.
So there is no repetition and the information just flows.
Those in a coherent, smooth manner, because it makes it easier for them to process the information in chunks.
So if we provide the full, complete answer to a question in a single paragraph, then it makes it friendlier to LLMs to parse and get that information.
Another thing is writing for natural language processing.
For example, if we have an H2 that says, like, how does AI benefit cyber resilience?
Just a very basic example.
The first sentence in that paragraph should say, AI benefits or provides advantages for cyber resilience in the following way.
So it's kind of like repeating the question in the H2 in the first paragraph.
That kind of is also an easier way for LLMs to discover information.
And then also from a strategy perspective, the way that we use these clusters.
For a reason, because it allows us to build authority in specific buckets.
And the more content you have on your site in these specific buckets, the more the LLMs kind of perceive you as a knowledge base.
And they have more content to reference and they are more likely to use you as a citation source.
We found that we have some cybersecurity clients that have thousands of blog pages on their site.

And their citation rate, as we're tracking it in LLM Visibility Tools, is around 15%, which is a lot higher than the average 5% that we see for our other clients that don't have a lot of educational blog content on their site.
And then finally, yeah, I think actually, I think that those are the main points that I wanted to make.
So it's actually how the content is written.
has to be written well.
That's the point I'm trying to make.
Yeah, that's great.
And then I guess to that line, do we have, do you have a baseline of like how we're showing up?
Yes, do.
We track your AI visibility in Scrunch.
can send a PDF version of that report later.
Great.
That would be awesome.
Nick and I have a meeting tomorrow where it would be helpful to have that information.
Yeah.
And the very last thing, I know we're at time.
We have a campaign kicking off at the beginning of January.
The theme of that campaign is like attack resilience.
I don't know how you think about, like, we'll have three, like, primary campaign themes throughout the course of the year.
I don't know how you're thinking about that in terms of the topic clusters.
I'd say three and a half.
The half is like AI, which is going to be like kind of the common thread across all of it.
So I don't know if we should think about adding another topic cluster that's like security, AI and security.
cybersecurity, whether it's kind of both AI as like an attack vector as well as AI as a defense vector, that might be something to think about.
And then I know I just rambled a lot, but how do you think about campaigns in terms of clusters?
Yeah, any campaigns that you're planning to run, those are also helpful for keyword research.
We might be able to fold in.
It just depends on the size of the opportunity.

So if there's a lot of potential keywords that we could target within that campaign, then we can absolutely build like a whole cluster and just like publish like 20 plus pieces of content that just talk about that particular topic.
It would be helpful if you could share like as you develop that material internally, you can send it to us and then we kind of proactively start thinking about it.
But yeah, the clusters aren't like set in stone so we can build on them and like add additional ones.
As you know, the need emerges.
Yeah.
Great.
Yeah.
So we'll have the, the campaign messaging, I think, done by the end of this month, or into the first week of next month for the first one, which is business resilience, or is it tech resilience?
The next one will be, I think, business resilience.
And the third one is identity resilience, but those are not coming until Q2 at the earliest.
And then, since AI is such a focus, like, we should probably make something around AI and security one of the topic, one of the topic clusters, cluster topics, whatever they are.
Okay.
Okay.
Yeah.
Yeah.
That sounds good.
Sorry for creating more work.
No worries.
We do always need new content, so it's just planning ahead.
Great.
Yeah.
Thank you.
All right.
Well, thank you.
I will send a follow-up email.
See ya.
Bye.
Bye.
Bye.
Bye.
Bye.
Bye.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Sync on content strategy and align on next steps.](https://fathom.video/share/2WSdYfjCUh-xF785yu1-y47j4crxrBzR?tab=summary&timestamp=141.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Production Paused:** Content production is paused until PMMs deliver all product documentation (due EOW) and approve topic clusters (due EONW).](https://fathom.video/share/2WSdYfjCUh-xF785yu1-y47j4crxrBzR?tab=summary&timestamp=781.0)
  - [**PMMs Get Airtable Access:** Garrett, Joe, and Jen will receive Airtable access to provide direct feedback on topics and drafts, unblocking the review process.](https://fathom.video/share/2WSdYfjCUh-xF785yu1-y47j4crxrBzR?tab=summary&timestamp=436.0)
  - [**New Topic Cluster:** A new "AI and Security" cluster will be created to align with N-able's 2025 campaign themes, which include "Attack Resilience."](https://fathom.video/share/2WSdYfjCUh-xF785yu1-y47j4crxrBzR?tab=summary&timestamp=1758.0)
  - [**AI Disclaimer Removed:** The AI-use disclaimer will be removed from the style guide and all drafts, as it is unnecessary and not used on other N-able AI content.](https://fathom.video/share/2WSdYfjCUh-xF785yu1-y47j4crxrBzR?tab=summary&timestamp=1483.0)

## Topics

### Content Production & Review Blockers

  - [**Blocker:** Content production is paused pending two key inputs from the PMM team:](https://fathom.video/share/2WSdYfjCUh-xF785yu1-y47j4crxrBzR?tab=summary&timestamp=781.0)
    1.  [**Product Documentation:** Required for all products to build the Product Feature Matrix.](https://fathom.video/share/2WSdYfjCUh-xF785yu1-y47j4crxrBzR?tab=summary&timestamp=543.0)
    2.  [**Topic Cluster Approval:** Required for the five proposed topic clusters in Airtable.](https://fathom.video/share/2WSdYfjCUh-xF785yu1-y47j4crxrBzR?tab=summary&timestamp=498.0)
  - [**Solution:** PMMs will receive direct editing access to Airtable to accelerate feedback.](https://fathom.video/share/2WSdYfjCUh-xF785yu1-y47j4crxrBzR?tab=summary&timestamp=436.0)
      - [**Access Granted:** Elaine (Marketing), Garrett, Joe, and Jen (PMMs).](https://fathom.video/share/2WSdYfjCUh-xF785yu1-y47j4crxrBzR?tab=summary&timestamp=436.0)
      - [**Rationale:** Bypasses Studi's 2-week absence.](https://fathom.video/share/2WSdYfjCUh-xF785yu1-y47j4crxrBzR?tab=summary&timestamp=436.0)
  - [**Timeline:**](https://fathom.video/share/2WSdYfjCUh-xF785yu1-y47j4crxrBzR?tab=summary&timestamp=759.0)
      - [**EOW:** All product documentation delivered.](https://fathom.video/share/2WSdYfjCUh-xF785yu1-y47j4crxrBzR?tab=summary&timestamp=759.0)
      - [**EONW:** PMMs provide concrete feedback on topic list.](https://fathom.video/share/2WSdYfjCUh-xF785yu1-y47j4crxrBzR?tab=summary&timestamp=759.0)
  - [**Interim Plan:**](https://fathom.video/share/2WSdYfjCUh-xF785yu1-y47j4crxrBzR?tab=summary&timestamp=651.0)
      - [One of the five existing drafts will be edited using the new Cove documentation.](https://fathom.video/share/2WSdYfjCUh-xF785yu1-y47j4crxrBzR?tab=summary&timestamp=651.0)
      - [**Goal:** Use this draft to calibrate the PMM feedback loop for product accuracy.](https://fathom.video/share/2WSdYfjCUh-xF785yu1-y47j4crxrBzR?tab=summary&timestamp=683.0)

### Product Feature Matrix & Fact-Checking

  - [**Purpose:** The matrix centralizes product info for two uses:](https://fathom.video/share/2WSdYfjCUh-xF785yu1-y47j4crxrBzR?tab=summary&timestamp=928.0)
    1.  [**Fact-Checking:** Ensures accuracy in drafts against approved messaging.](https://fathom.video/share/2WSdYfjCUh-xF785yu1-y47j4crxrBzR?tab=summary&timestamp=931.0)
    2.  [**Topic Selection:** Informs keyword research and helps link articles to specific products.](https://fathom.video/share/2WSdYfjCUh-xF785yu1-y47j4crxrBzR?tab=summary&timestamp=931.0)
  - [**Status:** The Cove matrix is complete, built from docs provided by Garrett.](https://fathom.video/share/2WSdYfjCUh-xF785yu1-y47j4crxrBzR?tab=summary&timestamp=191.0)
  - [**Content Guidance:**](https://fathom.video/share/2WSdYfjCUh-xF785yu1-y47j4crxrBzR?tab=summary&timestamp=191.0)
      - [**Include:** High-level benefits (e.g., meeting cyber insurance requirements).](https://fathom.video/share/2WSdYfjCUh-xF785yu1-y47j4crxrBzR?tab=summary&timestamp=191.0)
      - [**Exclude:** Sales-focused competitive call-outs (e.g., "forced three-year contracts").](https://fathom.video/share/2WSdYfjCUh-xF785yu1-y47j4crxrBzR?tab=summary&timestamp=191.0)

### Content Strategy & Keyword Research

  - [**Keyword Research:**](https://fathom.video/share/2WSdYfjCUh-xF785yu1-y47j4crxrBzR?tab=summary&timestamp=332.0)
      - [**Action:** Research "business resilience" vs. "cyber resilience."](https://fathom.video/share/2WSdYfjCUh-xF785yu1-y47j4crxrBzR?tab=summary&timestamp=332.0)
      - [**Hypothesis:** Different search results → different user intent → targetable as separate topics.](https://fathom.video/share/2WSdYfjCUh-xF785yu1-y47j4crxrBzR?tab=summary&timestamp=393.0)
  - [**Airtable Topic Briefs:**](https://fathom.video/share/2WSdYfjCUh-xF785yu1-y47j4crxrBzR?tab=summary&timestamp=1353.0)
      - [**Action:** Add a "Topic Brief" field to Airtable.](https://fathom.video/share/2WSdYfjCUh-xF785yu1-y47j4crxrBzR?tab=summary&timestamp=1353.0)
      - [**Purpose:** Provide PMMs with context (e.g., target products, article direction) to facilitate efficient review.](https://fathom.video/share/2WSdYfjCUh-xF785yu1-y47j4crxrBzR?tab=summary&timestamp=1353.0)
  - [**New Topic Cluster: "AI and Security"**](https://fathom.video/share/2WSdYfjCUh-xF785yu1-y47j4crxrBzR?tab=summary&timestamp=1758.0)
      - [**Rationale:** Aligns with N-able's 2025 campaign themes ("Attack Resilience," "Business Resilience," "Identity Resilience") and the company's AI focus.](https://fathom.video/share/2WSdYfjCUh-xF785yu1-y47j4crxrBzR?tab=summary&timestamp=1758.0)

### Content Optimization for LLMs

  - [**Goal:** Structure content for better discoverability and citation by LLMs.](https://fathom.video/share/2WSdYfjCUh-xF785yu1-y47j4crxrBzR?tab=summary&timestamp=1534.0)
  - [**Tactics:**](https://fathom.video/share/2WSdYfjCUh-xF785yu1-y47j4crxrBzR?tab=summary&timestamp=1534.0)
      - [**Structure:** Add FAQs and "TLDR" summary boxes.](https://fathom.video/share/2WSdYfjCUh-xF785yu1-y47j4crxrBzR?tab=summary&timestamp=1534.0)
      - [**Readability:** Use clear H2s/H3s and unique, coherent paragraphs.](https://fathom.video/share/2WSdYfjCUh-xF785yu1-y47j4crxrBzR?tab=summary&timestamp=1534.0)
      - [**NLP:** Repeat the H2 question in the first sentence of the section.](https://fathom.video/share/2WSdYfjCUh-xF785yu1-y47j4crxrBzR?tab=summary&timestamp=1637.0)
      - [**Authority:** Build deep topic clusters to establish N-able as a knowledge base.](https://fathom.video/share/2WSdYfjCUh-xF785yu1-y47j4crxrBzR?tab=summary&timestamp=1674.0)
  - [**Performance:**](https://fathom.video/share/2WSdYfjCUh-xF785yu1-y47j4crxrBzR?tab=summary&timestamp=1735.0)
      - [**Metric:** N-able's AI visibility is tracked in Scrunch.](https://fathom.video/share/2WSdYfjCUh-xF785yu1-y47j4crxrBzR?tab=summary&timestamp=1735.0)
      - [**Benchmark:** Cybersecurity clients with deep content clusters achieve \~15% citation rates, far exceeding the \~5% average.](https://fathom.video/share/2WSdYfjCUh-xF785yu1-y47j4crxrBzR?tab=summary&timestamp=1701.0)

      - [Send Scrunch AI visibility report PDF to Andrew Beyer.](https://fathom.video/share/2WSdYfjCUh-xF785yu1-y47j4crxrBzR?tab=summary&timestamp=1735.0)
  - [**Andrew Proft:**](https://fathom.video/share/2WSdYfjCUh-xF785yu1-y47j4crxrBzR?tab=summary&timestamp=759.0)
      - [Push PMMs for artifact delivery (due EOW) and topic feedback (due EONW).](https://fathom.video/share/2WSdYfjCUh-xF785yu1-y47j4crxrBzR?tab=summary&timestamp=759.0)
  - [**Andrew Beyer:**](https://fathom.video/share/2WSdYfjCUh-xF785yu1-y47j4crxrBzR?tab=summary&timestamp=845.0)
